text.title: Requirements Validation and Sign-Off Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the requirements-validation methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (score attributes): Score each requirement on 10 attributes.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the score attributes gate.
Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the match technique gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (run segmented review): ≤200 reqs per session; capture dissent.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the run segmented review gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (sign off gate): Sponsor sign-off; gate to baseline.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the sign off gate gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for requirements-validation do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
